Do not use On Error Resume Next or On Error GoTo; these are legacy VB6 patterns that hide bugs.
Do not catch Exception or SystemException broadly unless you are at the top-level boundary (global handler) and you log/re-throw.
Do not use empty Catch blocks that silently swallow exceptions (Catch ex As Exception without any handling).
Do not use exceptions for normal control flow (e.g., catching an exception to check if a key exists in a dictionary; use TryGetValue instead).
Do not throw System.Exception directly; always use a more specific derived exception type.
Do not expose stack traces, connection strings, or internal details to end users in error messages.
Do not catch and re-throw with Throw ex as it resets the stack trace; use Throw alone.
Do not rely on Finalize/destructor patterns for resource cleanup; use IDisposable and Using instead.
Do not put large amounts of business logic inside Catch blocks; keep them focused on handling the error.
Do not ignore Task exceptions in async methods; always Await tasks or observe their exceptions.
